meaning: n.(猛)推,插,挤adj.有强大推进力的
examples: She broke free by thrusting her elbow into his chest.
Peter, angrily pushing the goats aside, marched up to Heidi, thrusting a letter into her hand.
Thrusting in the dark, the light is flown with dust.
But the Redskins weren't shy about thrusting their young leader out there as the answer.
At this thought, I am galvanized into action, thrusting clothes at Sarah with alarming ferocity.
